Definition: Skills address an abstract capability CATEGORY (~~tracker, ~~chat); a separate resolver table binds it to whatever MCP server the user connected, so one skill body is portable across competing tools. Distinct from CM-04 (lazy retrieval) and CM-25 (index) because the indirection is a vendor-abstraction layer, not just deferred loading.

A graded eval measuring the OS's OWN behavior — skill-routing accuracy, memory contribution (A/B phrase-subset), or agent-session quality from session traces — against labeled cases with a min-pass-rate CI gate. No existing CM/SK code captures evaluating the system's own retrieval/routing as a tested behavior.

100% of project state lives in an external file tree / issue tracker / addressed memory blackboard; agents hold no durable state and are hot-swappable, coordinating via filesystem mailboxes or addressed key-spaces rather than direct handoff. Generalizes the file-message-bus, issue-tracker-as-DB, blackboard, and receiver-tagged-routing flags into one architecture.

A concept/entity earns a durable first-class page only when independently mentioned ≥2 times across distinct sources; single-source mentions stay transient. Counting signal is the promotion gate. Inverse of CM-15 decay; distinct from CM-16 consolidation because it gates page CREATION, not merge.

Spend is a first-class governance gate: free-tier/model pins, a cost ledger, named retrieval cost-mode bundles, subscription-session runtime, and threshold alerts wired as halting checkpoints. No CM/SK code treats cost as a constitutional constraint.

A runtime check that proves work is genuinely done — sandbox re-execution emitting an unfakable ship token, reachability/orphan classification, per-phase typed-evidence PreToolUse gate — blocking scaffolded-but-unwired code from counting as shipped. Stronger than CM-22 written governance: it is a runtime oracle.

A CI script derives headline counts and cross-references from the actual directory tree and fails the build if committed docs/manifests disagree (or regenerates them from source and self-commits). Keeps a large monorepo's self-description from drifting. Distinct from SK-21 skill-quality validators because it polices the corpus's own bookkeeping/docs, not skill behavior.

A probabilistic harness runs many simulated invocations and produces confidence intervals + a certification grade, treating a skill like a flaky test suite. Distinct from SK-21 (boolean lint) by being a measurement discipline with CIs and grades.

Canonical_name: Domain re-skin of a coding-agent OS
Definition: Forking an entire spec-driven dev toolchain and rebinding its slots (tests→metrics, code→assets, constitution→domain principles) to a different function while keeping the CLI/command/template machinery intact. The OS is the fork-and-rebind recipe. Not captured by SK-16 (cross-harness) or SK-17 (bundling).

A synthesis pass over independent parallel outputs reports BOTH convergence (signal) AND unresolved tensions, refusing consensus — preserved disagreement is the deliverable, escalated to a human. Opposite of SA-08 (debate→consensus) and SA-18 (council to a verdict).

A gate selects WHICH specialist personas join a run based on detected signals (business type, MCP/credential availability, prior-state), and degrades the same workflow to single-context sequential when the harness lacks subagents. Distinct from SA-11 (waves of identical workers) and SA-05 (unconditional fan-out): it is per-run roster composition + graceful degradation.
Continuous quantitative per-agent trust/reputation scoring used to gate or weight an agent's contribution in coordination/consensus. No SA code (closest SA-08/SA-16) covers reputation weighting.
